page: Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the DCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache, with support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AES cryptography, and Intel Advanced Vector Extensions. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark evaluations position the DC16s_v3 in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a single-core stress-ng score of 1898.0 and a multi-core score of 30343.46. Memory bandwidth tests recorded 1687.72 GB/sec for cached read-only small blocks, while uncached large-block read and write speeds reached 143.02 GB/sec and 60.51 GB/sec, respectively. In application-specific workloads, the server demonstrated strong performance in Geekbench image processing and small-model LLM text generation. However, performance drops significantly for larger models, with 70B model prompt processing falling into the weak tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the DC16s_v3 offers a high memory-to-core ratio suitable for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of local storage. The lack of bundled local storage requires users to attach external premium storage, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. Given its balanced multi-core performance and 128 GB memory capacity, this instance is suited for database operations, software compilation, image processing, and moderate-scale web serving. It is less suitable for large-scale LLM inference or workloads requiring high-speed local scratch disks.
description: The Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DCSv3 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM. The hardware configuration lacks local storage and GPUs, relying entirely on external storage options. Benchmarks indicate average performance across standard compute and memory tests, with a Passmark CPU score of 30808.33 and a memory score of 2586.66. The instance shows strong capabilities in image processing and small-model LLM text generation, but exhibits weak performance on larger 70B LLM workloads. This server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and software compilation where local storage is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced performance for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 features 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 server offering a high memory-to-core ratio and balanced compute performance for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C physical cores and vCPUs
128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Strong performance in image processing and small-model LLM text generation
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and memory-bound workloads
